sections.paragraph: m., dust, specially small and fine (see my conjecture as to the etymology [under the verb]), such as is easily scattered by the breeze, Isa 5:24, and such as a horse raises in galloping, Eze 26:10. It thus differs from עָפָר thicker and heavier dust, Deu 28:24. “The dust of God’s feet,” used poet. of the clouds on which God treads, Nah 1:3, comp. שַׁחַק.
